key_quote: Non-negativity clamping gap is the most critical unresolved soundness issue: TT-SVD truncation produces negative probability entries, post-truncation clamping breaks contractivity, and the bound is not formally established.
rationale: The tensor-train approach for CSL model checking of CMEs is acknowledged as novel. Blockers are the clamping soundness gap, unbounded-until convergence, axis-aligned proposition restriction, practical deployment barriers, and lack of theoretical support for TT-compressibility. All depth/rigor issues. Category B.
